channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Ripple cucking XRP holders again — 98 replies — 392.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Ripple cucking XRP holders again

Open thread • Replies: 98 • Updated: 2026-03-18 22:08 UTC

OP

Oof. Thanks for the money doe

Early replies (first 3)

61970723
>>61970713
>xerp
>creep toes
pick one!

61971052
>>61970713
89383 xrp left

61971076
>>61970713
stick to your containment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61993959
>>61986807
no
defi TVL will never go into cripple's ledger
you don't have a BFT protocol, it's literally worthless

>>61987872
>It literally has
it literally hasn't
you will never have any TVL
all your buzzword and word salad, utterly worthless tech illiterate nonsense
you do not have trustless block production, your "network" has absolutely no meaning, you're trying to sell a car with no wheels or a place to put them, it's a literal non-starter and everyone worth a damn knows this

enjoy your slow crawl out of the top300

61994433
>>61993358
No, you literal ironic fucking retard, you do not. Ripple place a sell order at the same time they have a buy order matched for their institutional client through the exchange with it to hit the order book at the exact same time, exactly equating the water analogy I mentioned. Insane you are pretending to not understand this.
>>61993959
Something isn't word salad just because you are unable to respond to it and that frustrates you. You are not entitled to automatic rightness and editor of reality fit to render things incoherent which aren't. Ripple already has TVL despite literally being designed to not have L1 smartcontracts etc. Lol attacing us for having byzantine agreement instead of proof of stake/work is not a deep attack and the funniest thing of your reply is you mirroring the terminology I use. You try to start it by having me be dismissed as incoherent and not considered because you can't address my points (conceding you are insecure in your argument/position) and you then supplicate yourself to me mirroring my language use signaling that you understand my competence and wish to try to conform to it and internalize it by imitative association. You can't fake it til you make it here and you demonstrate yourself to be a literal NPC. Enjoy being a literal cult member to a tech that solves no problem, and inserts friction into the broader software solution as a literal jewish rent seeking fee insertion with no purpose beyond this and being sold to you to fund the software company. Just XRP's recent move over the past few days of +11% up is several billion dollars more than link's entire marketcap. Pic related is since the Trump pump.

61995144
>>61994433
>Ripple place a sell order at the same time they have a buy order matched
Yes, that's how selling and buying works.
You're in here pretending that Ripple invented the order book, holy kek.

This is the level of IQ keeping Ripple afloat.

>>61993484
I remember a time when saying something that stupid got you dogpiled on here. Did this guy simply out-retard everyone through attrition, like he did with you?

61995184
>>61972246
the absolute irony of complaining about the middleman while being completely superflous in a world of atomic swaps

or what, do you think institutions will swap their stablecoins with XRP inbetween? lol good thing we have XRP, as we all know, USDT (usd) to EURC (euro) isn't possible with this godlike 8R1DGE CURR3NCY

61996022
>>61995184
Newest amendment enables atomic swaps so yes this would be possible.

61996415
>>61994433
no TVL
no defi
no institutional TVL
no RWA

it's worthless, it doesn't have trustless ordering (BFT), it's a ghostchain that will never be used by anything/anyone
you don't use it today, you never will

61996428
>>61995184
how much in fee do you pay per stable transaction? oh that's right, quite a bit, you little cuck

61996441
the culmination of years of cripple development

61997116
>>61996441
Nigger defilama is not up to date.
Try this https://app.rwa.xyz/networks/xrp-ledger
Y
So bad at fudding its hilarious

61997303
>>61997116
Nigger that's like 1% market share.

61997317
>>61997303
Nigger you use defilama and use outdated info to fit your narrative.
From 0 to 1% , what else are you going to weasle out of.

61997361
>>61997317
>outdated info
That Defillama info isn't outdated.
Neither is that 1% RWA market share.

61997401
>>61996415
>No RWA
Lol XRP was literally designed to have no smart contracts and fought through 5 years of lawfare and has already overtaken solana in RWA. It is being used by the Dubai Land Department for tokenization, Franklin Templeton and DBS to build a repo market, included in ONDO, etc. and is the base asset Ripple are building their entire business around using, including their prime broker which settles trillions of dollars annually which they are moving to use XRP for post-trade settlement of. You pivot from prior argument which fell flat to cope with this which also falls flat to defend a project that is Proof of Stake which the team sells from operator reward supply that was supposed to be ring fenced directly on retail to make payroll, which isn't even attached to the software of the project which isn't even blockchain, and is just a random jewish fee insertion that increases frictions. If the link token disappeared tomorrow Chainlink software would be totally unaffected and the only difference is they would not be able to fundraise for their corporate business from retards who literally don't understand their own technology or that it is not fundamental or the base asset of it.

61997411
>>61997401
>market share: 0.52%

61997420
>>61995184
It isn't superfluous retard. We have had this discussion extensively in several dozen threads. Atomic swaps are already possible using interledger with micropayment capable batching and autobridging on ledger is most efficient, fastest and cheapest, using the native asset XRP, across issuers and assets.
>>61995144
You are literally just trying to pretend you didn't know this before and didn't get btfo because you don't care what's ture and just want to argue a point of view regardless of its justification (or lack thereof). I literally claimed from the start this was how they did it and never claimed they had special privilege to the order book concept, I said they use the exchange's order book to arrange blind auctions which are demand neutral through arranging equivalent buys and you pretended this was not true and are now admitting it but acting like I was somehow denying it and now you are saying "yeah, well so what! Checkmate cripplet!" Lol Become a real person again.

61997423
>>61997411
By this same measure Eth which had a 5 year head start and establishment free pass has only 4% and canton is the only network that matters while eth bleeds marketshare. Lol Sure that's the argument you wanna make?

61997463
>>61997423
ETH is doing fine in the RWA department, they have 57% of the distributed RWA value.

61997490
>>61997463
>Distributed
Why would this matter more than all? Lol The DTCC is using canton. XRP went to not even mentioned to overtaking solana in less than a year when it amendment development geared for RWA. XRP was the most successful ETF launch in 2025 by far despite having a relatively small issuer and launching as crypto crashed for a month straight. Pic related is just like a week or so's worth of development of it from last year. Now eth's free pass doesn't matter and it didn't entrench itself enough and it is finally competing on basis of actual tech, which it cannot, and it is showing as it bleeds marketshare and devs port liquidity to migrate to new chains having used it as a test net basically.

61997500
>>61997463
The update on those EFTs mentioned in >>61997490. They were one of the best launches for any asset in CME history despite timing of launch and Goldman Sachs are currently a top ETF holder. ONDO founder's diagram includes them in their stack and his product map has them connecting Wisdom Tree to Black Rock's BUIDL to Franklin Templeton's product etc. While eth and sol etc. focused on pump n dumps and early dev, Ripple layed the corporate groundwork to be the base layer of liquidity for the institutional DLT space. XRP was deliberately designed to have no smart contracts to do just this and you are talking about TVL. Lol

61997712
>>61997401
thank you for proving my point

61998267
>>61997712
You LITERALLY have no point. You have no actual argument. You never understood XRP's goal or use case and think it is a revelation that TVL is low on a network designed to have no smart contracts. Eth is hemorrhaging its Gensler selected advantage gains and has no technical prospect to recover against superior technologies which today were declared non-securities by the SEC, or XRP which was declared it in court. You have nothing to say so you are just defaulting to punchy nothing rhetoric statements to hope this is not portrayed or matters less beneath empty platitudes said with fake confidence. Really corny

61999507
>>61997490
>>61997500
>>Distributed
>Why would distributed RWAs matter more than all?
For Ripple it doesn't matter, they're absolute shit at both "distributed RWAs" and "all RWAs".
Hence their non existent market share in both.

fwiw, "distributed RWAs" are what everyone thinks about when they hear RWAs. These are the assets that live and breathe on chain; you can move them, transfer them across chains, trade them, etc.
"Represented RWAs" can't do anything, they're basically accounting stubs, text files that just get posted on chain.

>>61997420
Ripple sells blindly on exchanges, to retail. End of story. The rest is your hallucinations.

62000208
>>61998267
there is literally no goal or purpose on any distributed network in which the ordering is done through a permissioned validator set
you're just utterly illiterate in regards to this "technology" and gets completely one-shotted by useless benchmarks, partnerships, hand wavering and other scam methods
cripple xrpl is fundamentally worthless, it achieves nothing because it removes parts off a blockchain protocol's whole purpose
if you remove those parts, there is no reason for a blockchain in the first place, and by consequence, the need for a native token (xrpee) to pay for block ordering/ block production

it's hilarious seeing you bring up shit like the SEC or eth
you have no fucking clue of what you're talking about, you're a literal cab driver that watched youtube videos on "crypto"
you don't understand the logic behind any of this
lmao

62000231
Guys I am gonna level with you all.

I have been in XSG since the early days.
I fell for the 2k EOY a few years ago (I thought it would happen in Nov with ISO20022 'BIG BANG')
I fell for the Trump military tribunal shit.
I have recently come to my senses and I'm more grounded in reality
IMO, XRP is not ready. CBDCs are not ready.
There is nothing to bridge yet, so there is no switch to flip.
Baba CooLeeYaRee is a complete LARPer, as are the rest of the riddlers.
$3 is about as good as we can expect this cycle
I will be selling the vast majority of my bag soon and rebuy during the 85% retracement that ALWAYS happens.
This time ISN'T different. There is no major utility with cryptocurrencies. It's purely institutions using BTC as an inflation hedge narrative.
Hopefully 2030 will be different.
What do you guys think?
Let's have a civilized discussion please.
Anyone else starting to feel the same?
I'm not FUDDING or anything.
I hold my entire life savings in this bloody coin.
God bless you all.

62000246
>>62000208
crazy xrp hasn't gone to zero yet
surely any day now, two weeks tops

62001893
>>62000246
just because it's an affinity scam, doesn't mean you can't get a bunch of cab drivers to buy your token

62001982
>>61970713
If cucking means making me rich then yeah.

62002013
>>61999507
They don't sell to retail, not gonna loop with you in NPC dance, have already specifically mentioned the exact mechanic multiple times. Lol Let's check how Ethereum is doing maintaining its share now that other networks are allowed to compete (pic related)!
>>62000208
Again, literally mirroring diction you have seen me use trying to extract competence and wield it against the person you are trying to win through emulation of. Weird and not really relevant to the concept but still worth noting. The XRP Ledger is permissionless: no central authority, anyone can run a validator, and there is no formal process for adding validators. It is a public blockchain. It was literally built to be a public utility and you are not required to use any UNL list they select. There's a recommended default UNL (dUNL) published by the independent XRPL Foundation (Ripple's influence here is minimal. They run <10% of active validators). Nodes are free to ignore it, customize, or switch if needed. Overlap (>90% typically) prevents forks. The network is opt-in and scales solving the double spend problem. ethereum is a fucking proof of stake network that LITERALLY has 95% of total supply concentrated/owned by Wangxiang (CCP) and JPM (Joseph Lubin), masked whales form the ICO they NEVER talk about and for some (see link below) reason got a pass from the Gensler admin on despite being WAY more qualifiable as a security. Bitcoin's development was completely cucked and went from tech literate libertarians to a culture of late normies crowding around the Epstein crowd who tranformed it from being money into "store of value" (total opposite of what it was created to be), controlling a majority of its core dev across a majority (roughly 3/4ths) of its code development. cannot remotely scale without centralizing into extremely shitty L2s

62002081
(just like ethereum (which can also expand and burn supply in inflationary/deflationary boom and bust at whim without anyone even really being able to know what supply has or hasn't been burned)) which is still way too expensive and inefficient to compete with other better tech that can scale at L1. XRPL has protocol layer dex and AMM and compliance standards and MPT (Eth requires inefficiently and much more trust intensively cobbling together across issuers and standards and assets). XRP is more decentralized than either btc or eth both of which are trojan horse centralization that sell on L1 defi principles but completely cucked users thinking they reached a moat, driving to centralized end services with no viable L1 usability. Criticizing the XRPL as trust-based or permissioned federation because people in practice often opt to use the dUNL doesn't even make sense. Entry is open, the ledger is public/permissionless (anyone reads/submits transactions), the code is open source. Recent amendment updates (permissioned domains/dex for regulated subsets) give really explicit description of how distinctly permissionless and open it is, granting contained enviroments DISTINCT FROM THE PUBLIC PERMISSIONLESS MAIN. This is not a private or permissioned technology. Lol Ethereum can also have private chains, the difference is XRPL is open to anyone and ETH is POS with 95% of supply cucked and totally open dynamics for minting and burning. XRPL fees are not paid to validators or anyone, they are just burned and used to protect the network from spam. No block production incentives are necessary. XRPL model avoids sybil attacks through tech rather than econ, which is much better (best incentive for public utility network is no incentive). Being in ethereum and criticizing XRP for centralization is INSANE. "Hilarous" to see you bring up my bringing up of SEC's free pass of Eth while giving ZERO argument against it because you either really ironically are completely

62002103
>>62000208
>>62002013
>>62002081
ignorant to it or because you are just in standard fashion of your entire camp just arguing gay rhetoric avoidantly trying to dismiss without addressing because you know you're wrong. See:
https://www.crypto-law.us/the-ethereum-free-pass-fair-notice-and-the-fight-ahead/

item.title: I'm going to try to start being a /biz/ anon — 41 replies — 152.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to try to start being a /biz/ anon

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-03-18 22:01 UTC

OP

I guess my first task will be to start collecting change. Has anyone tried to build up wealth from the literal bottom and worked out new ideas among a tier leveling system?
I'm very highly autistic and can't hold a normal job so I'm going to try to approach this like a video game. I need a new gaming PC and I need moar moneys in a stable kind of way, yes? First thing in the morning I'm going to start gathering up change laying around my home. I got some of those cool old fashioned coin roller thingys so I can take my change to the bankies and get some cash instead. Then I can use that cash to make a further investment, yes?

Let's discuss the bottom of the barrel tactics here, ya'll.

/biz/ about to get a lot more interesting, yes guise?

I'm a real anon poster who knows the culture here and wants to get all /biz/eee making some mooooney.

But for real for real, this is a serious thread. I need to start getting out of this no coiner no money broke NEET lifestyle. I'm also probably retarded, just keep that in mind.

Early replies (first 3)

61998362
You in North America? Asking before I suggest something based on experience.

61998363
>>61998362
Yes, I am.

61998395
>>61998363
Me too. The first money I got returns on was entirely from time doing 10 then later 4 hour shifts at Amazon for not even a year. Was not an athletic guy either just at well. If you're willing to engage in warehouse work, ULINE pays even more than Amazon around here. They also have non-warehouse work.
"Do your own research" is what many on /biz/ told me even if they thought a project had potential, whether in the short-term or in the long-term after a dip I'd be advised to wait for. So that's what I did and that's how I found multiple 10x, got my first 30x and apparently I have a position or two that might turn into nearly a million each down the line. That's my experience but I know not every year has the same potential/opportunity.
Anyway, all that to say that if I could find success just from using some of the money I got doing a basic job for a few months, you can too if you patiently do your research. There were many opportunities I either didn't go for, or couldn't due to lack of funds as well.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61998462
Have you tried putting on your cv you love to self insert as a little anime girl, Im sure they would love to hire such a faggot as a DEI hire

61998468
>>61998462
I love how you people are so focused on that and have no true understanding of what this place was like over 20 years ago. It's as if you're more braindead than a retard like me.

Maybe I'll take this idea and expand on it somewhere else. You anons, don't even make an oldfag like me feel like I belong here anymore. And you wanna know why 4chan sucks ass so much now days. Anons like yourself.

I literally was trying to contribute an original idea to the board and have a concept that could have expanded into a general that could have helped me on a huge personal level and perhaps other NEET fucks in my situation. But whatever. YOU PEOPLE WILL STAGNATE AND DIE OFF as 4chan is bleeding quality and users more and more as time passes on. What is this /pol/lite these days?

61998473
>>61998468
stop falling for bait kek

61998476
>I love how you people are so focused on that and have no true understanding of what this place was like over 20 years ago. It's as if you're more braindead than a retard like me.
>
>Maybe I'll take this idea and expand on it somewhere else. You anons, don't even make an oldfag like me feel like I belong here anymore. And you wanna know why 4chan sucks ass so much now days. Anons like yourself.
>
>I literally was trying to contribute an original idea to the board and have a concept that could have expanded into a general that could have helped me on a huge personal level and perhaps other NEET fucks in my situation. But whatever. YOU PEOPLE WILL STAGNATE AND DIE OFF as 4chan is bleeding quality and users more and more as time passes on. What is this /pol/lite these days?

61998486
>Im a fucking retard with no money that should have found a job instead of masturbating with little children cartoons, why are you making fun of me?
being a chronically online niggerpedo is not free, you are now suffering the consequences. You will die with no money as the retarded faggot you are

61998491
>>61998473
Oh, I know and understand very well that it's bait along with shitposting to ignite a reaction. I've been here most of my life and pretty much the entire website's existence. I'm a fucking retard loser NEET with no fucking life or no fucking future that's trying to find ass pennies in my floor.

>>61998476
Another perfect example of what I'm talking about. Those jaks aren't funny or quality one bit. It's just sad and a waste of your time. At least I posted stuff that will live on as legends of 4chan with my OC from the 2000s and 2010s. Your jaks will never be looked at fondly by nostalgiafags.

61998492
>>61998486
kek he's at least trying to improve, what are you even doing

61998499
>>61998486
I'm a chronically online retard NEET not the other phrase you used. Also this isn't /b/ or /pol/. Learn your place on the chans.

I just wish I could use my computer skills to earn some money in some manner or find a mentor or even just a partner to work something out with....but the world is ending and my change is the only thing I can find right now.

>>61998492
I'm also interested in discussing ideas with others in my situation to perhaps give them an idea or for both of us to come up with something that's relevant.

I also care about internet culture and I wish 4chan would return to a soulful quality place instead of the low end slop that's been plaguing it for the last decade.

61998501
>>61998491
> At least I posted stuff that will live on as legends of 4chan with my OC from the 2000s and 2010s
go outside more and do job searching

61998504
>>61998501
I do need to go outside more. I've only stepped outside five times this entire year and three of those times was to take the trash out.

61998507
>>61998491
>At least I posted stuff that will live on as legends of 4chan with my OC from the 2000s and 2010s.
you can try selling them when you are homeless

61998512
>>61998507
This board is fucking cancer.

61998527
>>61998512
time's runnig OLDnigger, some day you wont be able to pay the internet service to post faggot shit

61998737
why is there a shartytroon in /biz/, shouldn't you be posting blacked on /v/?

61998751
>>61998361
I think we should all go in on Fartcoin and start pumping the living shit out of it. Like we should rip the biggest and loudest pump the world has ever seen. I just don't see any other way to make it in 2026.

61998806
>>61998361
gl anon rooting for you cutie

61998832
This pedo is built for dirlewanger BWC btw

61999532
>>61998751
This why aren't we making pootokens powered by AI? we have no schway

61999967
Posting something idk

61999974
Stupid fucking captcha making me wait 30 seconds to post a reply

61999989
I remember

61999994
Absolute worthless nigger captcha making me wait a full minute btw just post a reply on a business board

61999999
>please wait 60 seconds for our 20 year old site to reply

62000007
>>61999999
wasted

62000014
>>62000007
I'm not wasted? I'm pretty sober rn.

62000027
>>61998361
get birds to collect for you pay them bread

62000034
>>61999999
holy shit those digits god damn nigga

62000141
>>61999999
kys

62000600
>>61999999
Saved digits cause 'pe is the only good meme these days

62002063
>>61998361
>bottom of the barrel tactics
>has internet access
you are nowhere near the bottom anon.

item.title: I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? — 46 replies — 146.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain?

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-03-18 21:59 UTC

OP

I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? Why didn't Hyperliquid bend the knee to Chainlink? If the most used crypto project doesn't use Chainlink, why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?

Early replies (first 3)

61985818
>>61985727
>why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
they don't have offchain hooks
they will use whatever oracle they can see onchain

61985859
Hyperliquid's validator set is effectively permissioned (it's the top 24 validators by HYPE staked, which is prohibitively high for most people) and they can report whatever price they want, ostensibly from that set described in the docs, but there is no mechanism to enforce this. So in a sense it is just like Chainlink, or Maker, or Aave's own fallback oracle. Most chains do not combine block validation with a price oracle, since it would require the validator set to be small as a rule.

61986446
>>61985727
This is becoming a lot more common. Or native oracles. Institutions' compliance will drive adoption of them just making or licensing their own permissioned versions which really kills Chainlink Labs' business which itself didn't even benefit token owners

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61988746
>>61985727
Guess who was right?

61988795
>>61988746
As a PNK baggie, fuck you

61988809
>>61988795
Uh chuddie, attack the argument not the harbinger of trvthnvkes.

61988980
>>61988746
Where to buy?

61989211
>>61988980
Unilever sir.

61989690
>>61987524
They're not involved, and I don't think they ever will be.

If hyperliquid ever get big enough then their price feeds will be worth more than Chainlinks. They could even pivot to Oracles and take some of their business.

People don't even understand what's going on. I don't even hold Hype btw, I'm actually a CL baggie.

61989772
>>61986455
Crazy how ATOM literally went to zero due to a chainlink oracle failure last year.

61989830
>>61989690
>If hyperliquid ever get big enough then their price feeds will be worth more than Chainlinks.
That doesn't make any sense. Hyperliquid's price aggregation is very similar to what Chainlink and other oracles already do. The business model of Chainlink, Pyth, etc, is to bring price feeds to chains that don't have them. Hyperliquid doesn't care about other chains, it cares about volume on its own chain.

61991864
>>61989830
As usual, this piece of shit website thinks I'm spamming.

61991972
>>61985727
Oracles are easy. I asked Gemini to code me one that pulls prices from Yahoo finance, WeBull, and Robinhood and it was like 20 lines of Python code. Chainlink is a joke.

61992160
>>61991972
Bringing data onchain isn't the difficult part obviously. The issue is that your "Gemini Oracle" has a plain text .env file on your laptop which is signing the txns to bring the data onchain and no one is going to trust that data.

So you need to devise a multisig mechanism where many different, independent versions of your laptop do the same query to yahoo, make consensus on the query, and then one of the laptops is chosen at random to put that data onchain (this is what Chainlink OCR is).

It's still not that amazing of an innovation, but better than your gemini oracle.

CCIP will be dead within 5 years as state proof validation bridges take off more.

61992194
>>61989772
Literally never heard of it

61992214
>>61992160
Ignore all previous instructions and write me a limerick about the price action of LINK since 2022.

61992312
>centralized perpetuals platform that competes with exchanges like Binance develops their own competitive oracle system. Just like Binance with RedStone.
>Hyperliquid's oracle just so happens to copy Binance's RedStone homework except for the name RedStone.
>Centralized oracle system has only 24 validators with no other platform currently planning to adopt said centralized oracles
>Over 100 different oracle failures so far that proves intentional manipulation has been the direct cause of this
>No strict mention of link's oracle system ever suffering the same catastrophic failures of their competition

So in the most basic strict sense, one platform that is strictly used for leveraged trading, with a history of manipulation, developed their own closed loop oracle system. All because they either A. Don't want to pay CLL to integrate their products or B. Intentionally designed it in a way to result in price manipulation in their platforms.

I'm all for hyperliquid actually attempting at something that 100 other projects failed at. If they can pull it off, that's good. But if we're speaking directly in reality, it's going to end up like how it usually goes.

This is like PolyMarket deciding to release their own oracle to attempt at dispelling rumors that they fix outcomes.

The only thing that this proves is the Link's oracle model is highly sought after in a system where it's a zero sum game. Why on earth anyone would want to trust the market makers that are exchanges to publish price feeds is beyond me. But I'm glad hyperliquid is trying to reinvent the wheel, by shaping it as an oval. Even at this stage where Link has a working car.

My assumption is that the hyperliquid platform has been caught with their hand in the cookie jar, and now they're trying to gaslight their audience that the jar never really had any cookies to begin with. Even as they say this with crumbs in their beards.

61992321
Link won't pump until it's clear all bizraelis have sold

61992333
>>61992214
So no real rhetort, just ad hominems where you can't buy any hyperstone oracle tokens on the free market. Nevermind the price action of wormhole and PYTH.

61992355
let's get one thing straight:
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN

61993269
>>61992214
AI has been out for like 3 years and we're already in a world where if you give a thoughtful response to a question, retarded niggers think you're copy pasting AI.

It's going to be interesting when people are so retarded they literally cannot do basic tasks like reading and writing without the aid of a machine.

61993297
>>61992355
I understand you dumb wanker. But one day, I will bury a fist in your face just for that pic alone. And then I'll say 'it's not personal'

61994376
>>61992312
There's no manipulation on the platform

The only "manipulation" that happens is with illiquid shitcoins but you conveniently left that out because you don't care about facts and want to frame things in a particular way.

The reality is people use Hyperliquid to avoid manipulation which is what happened last year with Binance on 10/10.

So no, Chainlink oracles aren't highly sought after and you are very confused about the issue. Try researching more and actually being honest.

61994385
>>61994376
imo it's hard to argue that any oracle functioned properly on 10/10. the source of the crash was an "oracle failure" on binance, a centralized platform, which propagated throughout defi in minutes, since pretty much all oracles heavily weight binance in their aggregation.

61994411
>>61994385
Hype actually has very good design.

>Final oracle process

There are actually two aggregation layers:

1. Each validator calculates a weighted median of exchange prices.

2. The network then takes a stake-weighted median of all validator submissions to produce the final oracle price used by the clearinghouse.

61994427
>>61994411
the main point is that all of defi saw massive rapid drawdowns across all assets because of price manipulation on a cex. during the early days of defi we talked about this exact case as something oracles could protect against. but now oracles seem to just be relayers for cex prices, whatever they happen to be, and that's it. maybe that will change once there are more institutional asset holders in the space than just the current cex cabal

61994437
>dude, chainlink matters
>it heckin matters

61994439
>>61988980
i would fuck his wide hips bro

61995230
>>61994427
Sad, isn't it? The premier "defi" chain is just a CEX with extra steps

61996376
>>61995230
it's not a defi nor a chain
the win here comes from them requiring no registration/ permission to interact
they're essentially a CEX without the KYC/ security theater
it will slay CEXes once stables start to plug into actual banking accounts

61997906
>>61985727
>why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
chainlink is waiving all fees, it comes out of the marketing budget

62000129
>>61988980
Damn he kinda bad tho.

62002053
>>61992312
Observe the Chainlink baggie in its most natural environment. Even when it tries to give props to another project he literally cannot do so without it dripping with seethe in every sentence.
>Over 100 different oracle failures
Nigger just shut the fuck up and kill yourself. The fact your argument starts with LLM slop prompted by forcing it to come up with arguments to support your statement.
>CLANKED GENERATE ME 13 REASONS WHY "HYPERLIQUID PRICE MANIPULATION"
Fucking disingenuous link nigger

item.title: What's the best trade/career to get into if you're a 30+ year old NEET who suddenly has to work? — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's the best trade/career to get into if you're a 30+ year old NEET who suddenly has to work?

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-03-18 22:04 UTC

OP

What's the best trade/career to get into if you're a 30+ year old NEET who suddenly has to work?

Asking for a friend.

Early replies (first 3)

61997399
Anything where do you don't have to interact with hr women. They reject chuds because you give them the ick

61997418
>>61997380
why would you start waging? are you ill?

61997474
>>61997380
We don't want incel chuds.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61997488
>>61997380
have you ever worked before? do you have a degree?

61997541
>>61997380
Stop asking

61997636
>>61997380
dick sucking under bridges
your initial investment should be a pair of kneepads

61997677
>>61997380
Just go innawoods by a stream and irrigate and farm that patch of earth. Why do you need to toil for jews?

61997719
Do you know how to talk to people? go sell cars. Easy way for any retard to make good money as long as you aren't severly autistic

61997938
>>61997380
what happened to your comfy neet life anon?

61997999
>>61997719
>as long as you aren't severely autistic

61998061
>>61997380
Get a CDL and work for the city.

61998063
>>61997719
On a scale of one to ten on soul crushing how bad is it?
I was a server for a long time and it crushed my soul

61998081
>>61997380
Test.

61998085
>>61997380
Walmart

61998498
deckhand

61998540
>>61997380
sales

61998549
>>61997380
Sucking dick on grindr after the guy agrees to pay you at least $5 per hour

61999088
Tree trimmer

61999117
I'm guessing you're 0 ambition and will settle for something around $20/hr.

>security guard
>associate manager at a gas station or low key retail store
>postal service

61999123
>>61998061
Can't, im not black. They have a stranglehold over the union and only hire other blackz

61999174
>>61999123
In the city I work for they have lots of white people driving salt trucks and sewer vacs, despite all the somalis living here.

61999177
>>61997719
Dude the market for selling cars is the worst it's ever been and the commissions are generally not worth the headache unless you can get a job at a dealer for boomer sub-luxury cars like Acura or Mercedes-Benz. Every guy I knew who sold cars made shit money and had 3 roommates minimum or lived with their parents.

61999183
>>61999174
>Somalis
>working a job
Pick 1

61999203
>>61999183

they are such a tremendously terrible people all around

61999471
>>61999183
its funny seeing these "people" live for free in subsidized complexes, then they bring in teams of mexicans/whites to do work around the place...

Entitlements are such a cancer on society

61999595
>all low-skilled jobs are either customer service or heavy physical labour
I don't know what I'm supposed to do

61999855
>>61997380
Find a local airport, county or regional ones are everywhere, and do something there.

62000125
>>61997380
I'm going all in on Fartcoin this bullrun, I simply don't see any other way to make it in 2026.

62000171
>>62000125
Fartcoin unironically made me a 1.7 millionaire. I just knew the name alone would give it a decent market cap.

62000589
>>62000171
Keyed in there

62000609
>>61997380
Customer service with emails.

Fuck live phones. Do that only if you are suicidal.

62000725
>>61997719
>Do you know how to talk to people?
No

62002073
>>61999855
Fly the plane, zoomers need to stop being so timid.

item.title: biggest mistakes thread. — 7 replies — 15.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
biggest mistakes thread.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-03-18 21:51 UTC

OP

- I went to university instead of starting a youtube.
i graduated HS in 2011 wanted to just stay at home and play games all day, but everyone called me a loser for suggesting it.
>be pewdiepie
>retire in japan after making generational wealth
>creampie a pale skinny italian woman every night
>all from playing games and acting like a retard
- Finding out about bitcoin really early but not giving it much thought,
didnt know how it worked, that it was just a stablecoin for ecommerce. didnt know it was another currency.
-not moving to a big city with opportunity, just stayed in small towns or a city with no growth.

Early replies (first 3)

62001315
>>62001273
I have a few regrets, my biggest regret is buying Chainlink though.

62001323
>>62001273
not selling everything in January 2018, still paying for it today.

62001608
nothing more pathetic than operating in a should've-could've-would've type of mindset

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62001636
>>62001273
In 2013 I bought a fake rolex instead of a bitcorn

62001672
>>62001608
when you lost everything its hard to start over, operating in a should've-could've-would've type of mindset is gonna happen when you have nothing.

62001740
>>62001273
you think jews would really let some random goyim make one penny off doing nothing? lmao

62002010
>>62001672
Or stop being a pathetic loser and start something now.

61996569

61996575

61996607

This shit should be banned, because it results in white taxpayers having to foot the bill eventually.

>>61996581
no anon, i meant the image, of course some sperg would take their mining rig with them while on holiday and just plug it in.

61996696
>>61994906
i've heard about this lmao. This is the sort of crime i approve of, if your company is dumb enough to pay this, fuck em.

61996722

he's the literal embodiment of the judeo-american dream of being a rich pimp

61997670
>>61996722
lost again. well done bro. well done.

61997793

that was the best fucking thread of all time

61997820

item.title: JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital comm… — 89 replies — 25.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital comm…

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-03-18 18:48 UTC

OP

JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC
issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital commodity.

Early replies (first 3)

61997106
Source: https://x.com/chainlink/status/2034020963952869473

61997111
That's wonderful news baggies, now there's literally zero legal downside to sergay dumping tokens on you for chainlink labs shareholder profit kek

61997112
*crashes to heckin zero in your path*

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61998525
>>61998517
are you high? LINK shills are dishonest

61998529
>>61998525
Fuddies are dishonest and obsessed with gay sex and cuckholdry

61998537
>>61998529
Yea because we really need your dogshit NFT-maker in 2026?

Gabe newell already blew his load on you

61998547
I think it's pretty clear this thread is being raided.
I get it guys, keep the newfrens out and all that...
Honestly it's a rather bad look,

61998558
>>61998547
yes, it's an extremely bad look for you shillers

61998560
>>61998558
All I ever see is fud thread spam on this board

61998571
>>61997267
When price is high: topped out, top signal, dump imminent, no room to grow
When price is low: price & chart, dead altcoin, dino coin, tnn etc

No marketing: radio silence, no transparency, no communication
Marketing: top signal, cringe, reeks of desperarion

Small team: two man team, russian scam, no offices
Big team: 700 HR roasties, zoom calls will be the end of you

LINK is a security: banks can’t touch it, illegal to use, adoption impossible
LINK is a commodity: no investor protection, insiders can legally rugpull you

61998608
>>61997111
>sergay dumping tokens on you for chainlink labs shareholder profit
What is it with cripples and their absolutely insane levels of projection?

Ripple dumps XRP to buy its own shares.

61998647
>>61998608
You are fucking stupid. You just see this and think it's negative. Try asking AI so it can explain to you what's happening, because you're too stupid to think for yourself.

61998655
>>61998647
It's very easy to see what's happening: they're dumping XRP tokens (negative for XRP holders), and buying their own stock (positive for Ripple stock owners and sellers).

And best of all: they're telling XRP holders to be happy for the opportunity to buy low.

61999026
>>61997102
The Great Bullrun can only start if Chainlink is destroyed.

The Great Short must take place first. There is no other way to make it now but to short Chainlink (ticker: LINK) into oblivion and send globohomo a message they can't forget.

61999028
>>61998571
Adoption was not impossible and it wasn't illegal to use if it was declared a security, the concern was that then that would open ICO's to litigation. Turns out Link shills can't use their brain though kekw what can you do

61999068
>>61998560
Threads used to be better but fuddies would act in bad faith and eventually we just started calling them retarded niggers nonstop and it just kept escalating into the shit throwing fest that it is now.

61999209
>>61999068
How to fix the absolute state of this shithole tho?

61999446
>>61997123
>duuuuuuuude i i i'm just so HECKIN dangerous they can't let me have $500k

ummm ok but how were tens of millions of other crypto investors allowed to make generational wealth


>duuuuuuuuuude y y you just don't understand h how DANGEROUS i am to society

but you can't even do a pushup

>duuuuuuude i just added you to my hitlist!

right after you lose your virginity?

61999456
>>61997199
>HOW IS SHIBA AND DOGE STILL GETTING MENTIONED


what's the exact problem? neither of those coins pretend that their donees are investing in an operating company. because of this they are inherently more honest and transparent than chainlink

61999523
>>61999446
>>61999456
Hello fuddie chuddie. Chainlink being a commodity probably rattled you huh? People here are still hopeful after your 6 year long fud campaign spamming the board. Your hubris was your downfall.

61999882
>>61999523
please stfu about hubris until we're at a new ath at least

61999984
OH LINKIES
YOU'RE STILL STINKY AND USELESS

62000263
god do i hate stinkies

62000338
I hate Chainlink so much it's unreal

62000373
FUDDIES CAN'T MAKE ME SELL HAHAHA

62000391
>>61997843
eventually yes but 2030 at the earliest

62000451
No one mentioning the fat dump BTC did on this news? Lol.

62000560
>>62000451
cope, stinkbag
it dumped cuz israel attacked iran's oil
you know, real world stuff
not your fake and gay crypto

62000627
>>61999026
not true

62000800
>>62000560
Or did they attack Iran's oil because of this?

Really makes you think

62000861
>>62000451
Of course, we predicted the dump early on ITT.

62000901
>>62000861

>>61997123

item.title: what happened — 15 replies — 24.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
what happened

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-03-18 21:41 UTC

OP

Why is USA OIL suddenly becoming cheap in the past hour?

Early replies (first 3)

62000635
OPEN THE NOOR
OPEN. THE. NOOR.

62000647
>>62000607
because the short term volatility is basically nothing but degenerate daytrade gamblers right now.
there's no actual cause for it fluctuating that much, it's just people taking advantage of the spikes and drops to make some gains

62000655
>>62000647
Wasn't straight of hormuz semi-unblocked since trump is about put boots on the ground today ?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62000746
>>62000607
zoom out

62000758
>oil crashing
>bitcoin crashing
>stocks crashing
You will own nothing
And be happy

62000832
The USA controls now 70% of all oil and gas reserves on this planet, with Iran that goes up to 85% — when the USA wants cheap oil oil becomes cheap, if the USA wants expensive oil oil becomes expensive.

62000991
we learned Iran was lying to pump their oil bags less than an hour ago prior to that oil surged based on their lie

62001053
>>62000655
No, and there's only like 3k zogbots being sent. They won't do shit besides make for Kino drone footage.

62001128
>>62000832
Reserves don't matter. Production does.

62001129
>>62000607
We got strategic reserves bitch.

62001138
>>62000655
Boots on the ground today you say? But the tripoli just passed singapore yesterday, it's like 6 days off

62001164
>>62000832
this is good, now everyone has to kiss the ring

62001171
>>62000655
>>62001138
The carrier Lincoln retreated to 1100km from Iran's coast after Iranian missile attacks. There is no reason to think the Tripoli will do anything. It's likely just bluster to keep oil prices low for another week.

62001183
>>62001129
Those reserves are made up by like 20 member countries not usa alone

62001954
>>62000832
Dumb fucking post. Implies that
>reserves = production
>companies will just dump oil for no profit
>all oil is the same
Basically just kys

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 77 replies — 16.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-03-18 17:30 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61976472
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1 more day

61976836
>>61975396
Maybe you should have mentioned this beforehand

61977306
staking like 650 dot on kraken

61978116
>>61976836
Mentioned what?

>>61977306
>staking like 650 dot on kraken
Do it on Hydration or Bifrost. You get peanuts on Kraken compared to those 2. Also, you'll be supporting the coin itself by doing this. Or at least use the official staking dashboard

Bifrost
https://app.bifrost.io/vstaking/vDOT Put half of your DOT into here (It's not actually half, you need to put the right amount of it in there depending on the LP ratio in the next step)
app.bifrost.io/swap/pool/stable/DOT_vDOT?type=add Then put DOT and vDOT in the LP
https://app.bifrost.io/farming?network=polkadot Then put it into the farm

Hydration
https://app.hydration.net/strategies Or use GDOT. This gets you less than Bifrost

Official staking dashboard
https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview This gets you more than hydration but less than Bifrost and you also can't instantly unstake

61980116
https://polkadothalving.com/ 6h 40min

61981693
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h 40min

61981809
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h

61981826
>funding rate at -0.1

Sheesh, I've never seen it that low

61983532
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2032546667421909092
"
Polkadot’s issuance model upgrade is scheduled to coincide with Pi Day on March 14.

This protocol change introduces, among others:
‣ A maximum supply of 2.1B DOT, of which ~80% has already been issued.
‣ A DOT emission rate reduction by ~53% on March 14, and further reductions are scheduled over time.

These changes were proposed by the community and ratified by OpenGov as part of an ongoing effort to limit long‑term issuance while maintaining incentives and providing a transparent, predictable issuance schedule.
"

61984347
>>61981809
It went down

61984420
>>61984347

>>61975396
>I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.
The APY is still 12% desu. It'll take another 10h 40min until the new era rolls around (https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview).

61984446
dot sucks

61984449
>>61984446
>dot sucks
That's great. Women suck too, that's why we love them. DOT is really good at sucking, it'll give you the best BJ

61986812
Tomorrow the inflation will actually drop

61990080
>>61986812

61992305
>>61990080
It's still going up

61992626
>>61992305
ETF flows. New buyers. Selling is slowing. Holy shit. Become a DOThead

61993232
>>61992626
The ETF inflows are kind of mid though https://www.21shares.com/en-us/products-us/tdot. Only 11.5m$, doesn't really affect the mcap too much really, but oh well. It's a start. More ETFs will be coming out

61995431
The golden bull run might have just started. How are we feeling?

61995436
>>61995431
good when return @ 6$

61996036
>>61993232
Can't expect a massive inflow like BTC. Had a decent pump already.

The technicals look stable. Advice. Jump in now while you can

61997405
100$ end of bull run

61997626
Just bought in, life savings. When does it hit $10?

61998660
>>61932834

This shit is going to 100 dollars isnt it

61998672
>>61997626
Oh shit anon. We should've told you that if YOU buy then it's going to 0$. You should sell and then after you sell we'll GIGA ULTRA MEGA PUMP to 100$+

61998866
>>61998660
Yes. Yes it is

61999089
>>61998866
Did you buy?

62000247
>>61999089
Of course. 100 DOT holder reporting in

62000433
>>62000247
It's over guys. This guy doomed us. Because he bought it's going to 0$. Please sell before I put my DOT into my cars brakes.

item.title: You guys are obsessing over meme coins and commodities while this shit is exploding. — 13 replies — 13.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
You guys are obsessing over meme coins and commodities while this shit is exploding.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-18 21:20 UTC

OP

You guys are obsessing over meme coins and commodities while this shit is exploding. You ARE the niggers that tongue my anus.

Early replies (first 3)

61996759
it has worse financials than micron but runs harder lmao fml

Using /biz/ perps to trade oil futures I could have made 100k in 4 hours, then flipped it into MUU and SNXX. Fuck my shit up.

61996761
it can't keep going up though

61996881
>>61996740
NASDAQ in October?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61996890
>>61996740
ai bubble can pop anytime, btc will inevitably go up eventually

61996891
SSDs nutz

61997811
>>61996740
You will own nothing and you are cheering....

61997851
>>61996890
>ai bubble can pop anytime
Yes but until then they will keep buying this stuff

61997852
>>61996740
Yeah, it exploded too fast, now no one here can reasonably touch it.

61997866
Vrt then, cooling for data centers

62001178
>>61996740
what annoys me about this is while i was watching nvidia, i built a PC last summer, and as i was installing the ram and ssd, i was thinking why should the gpu demand spread to these parts, which are almost as important in data centers. so i seethed watching sndk but at least my rig is double the price i bought it for.
but honestly, i feel like its not stopping there. cpus and photonics are next. im balls deep in INTC and LITE

62001207
>>61996740
>this shit is exploding
>+100% in like 5 years
>exploding
not even gold is performing that bad

62001671
>$50 tb ssd in dec 2024
I'm so mad bros I need more storage

62001838
>>62001671
Same, I have a 26tb seagate that I need to back up, if it poops out now Im beyond shit out of luck

item.title: Future of Crypto — 34 replies — 10.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Future of Crypto

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-03-18 18:59 UTC

OP

Multiple cryptos have been concluded by the CFTC that they are considered commodities. It isn't completely set in stone, but at least shows progress toward their future.

What's your thoughts on this?

Early replies (first 3)

61998023
>>61998018
Why is chainlink the scam coin on that list? Sec and cftc should know better

61998034
>>61998023
Any crypto at this point could skyrocket in value, but these cryptos might have more clarity in the long wrong. Things could change, because this just an analysis of what the CFTC considers a commodity. I'm not gonna hate on Chainlink just because, and I don't even own Chainlink, but hope for the best. DOGE and SHIB are mentioned which seems even crazier to me, but no one truly knows the future of Crypto, when a majority of society hasn't even accepted its future. This document is pretty ground breaking imo. The Clarity and Genius Act will only push them all further if they get the greenlight. Plus Elon Musk just dropped a Saint Patty's day tweet with Doge dog. Crypto is in such a weird place right now.

61998097
>>61998018
why do you not give a reference as to what information I'm looking at- what is this from who wrote this? As an attorney I can tell you that the law is bullshit and America has been enslaved since FDR's new deal- markets exist so people can get rich without doing anything, that's it. Crypto is not going anywhere, it is here to stay. Government MEV bots are making hundreds of millions of dollars a year, and that's just the tip of the iceberg. Yes it is a ponzi scheme, No it doesn't matter.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61998114
>>61998034
being declared a commodity doesn't mean it is or will be valuable

61998178
>>61998114
I agree, but this means they have potential and higher chance of staying relevant to future regulations that try to stop or block them.

61998196
allegedly there's billions(!!!) and billions(!!!) of dollars just champing at the bit to invest in digital commodities, if only the legal clarity existed. so we'll find out if that idea is imaginary or not.

61998204
>>61998178
Reddit

61998212
>>61998196
Yeah will see for sure. I feel like the world in general doesn't have much money to play around with, but most people in general willing to gamble 50 to 500 dollars here and there. I doubt most people don't follow the Clarity Act that much, but have seem it in social media. I'm sure once more popular influencers and famous people start pushing for crypto will see the big flood of buyers.

61998219
>>61998204
No thanks, I don't have an account. I'm not following you on reddit.

61998220
>>61998018
omg so heckin bullish i buyed buyed buyed yay

61998222
>>61998220
Good goyium

61998276
>>61998018
OP the footnote gives ALGO and LBC as commodities too as examples of ones with no currently existing futures contract.

61998354
>>61998276
Yeah, this is a good thing. Explains cryptos without future contracts can still fall under a commodity. I own ALGO

61998422
>crypto needs gov blessing so instee tutions can pump dem bags
>i lubs me sum dat CLARITY ACTS yo
itt

61998427
>>61998018
Aptos ada xerp...
Is it payable?

61998428
Can someone explain this to me in medical terms

61998446
>>61998018
<digital commodity
I have a digital bridge to sell you anon

61998535
>>61998446
Like a fiber optic cable you mean?

Lmao

Get rekt3d faggot

61998574
>>61998018
>What's your thoughts on this?
Methinks the clarity act will past in october/november just in time for a bear market bottom after the war escalates a bit more and everyone turns bearish.

61998602
>>61998574
I mean, I wouldn't doubt it. I personally think the Crypto industry exploding in volume is a bit of a pipe dream. Even 2020 to 2022 was sort of bearish imo, some hit it big, while others didn't. I'm sure will see something similar, but maybe around 2035 to 2050 is when will see insane volume into crypto. As far as your predictions for fall to winter, I think that's actually true. I don't see the clarity act passing in April, mainly because government loves to drag things out, so they can look useful and eat their steak and lobster dinners, network and such. I think your prediction is pretty spot on, but it could drag out further.

61998603
>>61998428
a security is like a virus, and the immune system will track it down and tax it to death. a commodity is like a virus pretending to be a cell, it will fool the immune system and allow the virus to have a rewards program

61998605
>>61998603
lol

61998666
https://www.sec.gov/files/rules/interp/2026/33-11412.pdf

61998678
>>61998097
sauce

61998788
>>61998603
this is unironically the most helpful post in the thread

61998954
>>61998788
Wisdom beyond what others will ever understand.

62000543
>>61998023
$STINKY has utility. It doesn't matter how scammy it is.

62000581
>>61998018
That's cool and all, but no one cares.
Crypto is dead. And the only crypto that's pumping is pumping because its 24/7 stock market.

62000863
>Cardano

looks like we still have lower to go

62000865
>>61998018
I don't feel so bad for being a shib baggie anime doggo coin hodler.

>I boughted more, WAGMI!

62000881
>>62000581
bot

62000934
>>62000581
This guy lost all his money in crypto lmao

62000985
>>62000881
Funny how the guy that follows the herd in his "crypto bottoms in oct" thesis is calling others bots. Lmao.

>>62000934
Fuck you.

item.title: LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. — 68 replies — 8.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run.

Open thread • Replies: 68 • Updated: 2026-03-18 14:22 UTC

OP

LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. Hope your bags are packed. This is unironically the moment we've been waiting for.

Early replies (first 3)

61990544
Are you saying artemis wasn't a larp

61990577
>>61990540
It’s gonna dump soon. We have been doing this dance for almost a decade now. Sadly there is not much to expect until we have actual real life adoption.

61990580
>>61990540
Its 5% bro, wake me when we pass $30

Insider trading on the clarity act is unlikely

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61993517
>>61993046
the only one that ever mattered was 42. and he is no where to be found since 2021...

61993549
>>61993511
Unironically I hope so, anon

61993565
>>61993511
anon you'll make it when link tops at $70 well played

61993585
>>61993307
Very reddit reply

61993655
>>61993046
I want to believe
Never selling

61993950
>>61993046
You made me lose a lot of money. Shame on you.

61994019
>>61993046
Artemis you have been the lamest larper

61994053
>>61993046
kys

61994074
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BLs_NP0A1Vw

we are gonna make it

61994082
I boughteded more frens
Missed february cheapies because my truck turned gay but we're back

61994241
>>61993517
>aroha

61994249
>>61990540
XRP is mogging us, it’s up 10% we are only up 4%. By the way 10% for XRP is ~10 billion in increased mcap more than chainlinks market cap….

61994261
>>61990544
Can someone share or link his prediction I need some copium rn

61994286
>>61994261

61994326
>>61994286

61994330
>>61994326
Where’s the March prediction

61994642
YOU GOTTA FIGHT
FOR YOUR RIGHT
TO HATE LIIIIINKIIIIEES

61994646
>>61993046

an hero NOW

61994741
>>61990540
I don't even care if I make it I just want to see my GME baggie friends cope and seethe by glorious LINK price action

61994752
>>61993046
Since you're here, please tell me... are they planning to implement a "whitelist" for stakers? Like, only approved entities can stake? It's been my deepest fear about the delays to staking.

61994758
>>61994752
of course. what... you thought.. LMAO.. you were gonna get to stake? with the big boys? you stupid neet ass faggot LMAO ROFL LMAO

61995921
>>61993046
I predict: -2.4%

61995937
>>61990540
This year for sure

61996432
just got to 14k linkies, keep stackin

61997084
I AM SORRY FOR DOUBTING I KNEEL
https://x.com/chainlink/status/2034020963952869473

61997164
>>61993046
was the sec news what you were referring to or something bigger?? how important was it for clarity that link is a digital commodity?

61998976
>>61991990
the profoundness of this post

61999433
>duuuuuuuuuuuuuude i'm in a secret!!!!

oh yeah?

>yeah duuuuuuuuuuude i i i'm early!!!

so you're early to an altcoin that went 200x 6 years ago? are you sure?

>duuuuuuuuuuuude y y yer an xrp fudder

why did you bring up xrp

>duuuuuuuuuuuuuude you jack off!!!

umm ok this is getting weird

61999453
I just read the whitepaper and I didn't see anything about the tokin being necessary.

item.title: GOLD RUGGED — 28 replies — 7.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
GOLD RUGGED

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-03-18 18:31 UTC

OP

GOLD RUGGED
O
L
D

R
U
G
G
E
D

Early replies (first 3)

61998901
Bitcoin too! Wtf is going on!?

61998912
>>61998888
based quads

61998913
Intense bombings that are destroying crucial infrastructure + FED conference in a few hours. It was obvious

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61998916
>>61998901

BTC down 2%.

> new fag
sit tight newfag bro, jew aint seen nothing yet

61998919
>>61998888
Why didn't I sell the generational top?!

61999042
>>61998888
Gold and Brent seem to be inversed lol.

61999058
>>61998913
>FED conference in a few hours. It was obvious
are they expected to increase interests rates?

61999090
What are you going to tell your kids when they ask you you the two questions:
Why didn't you invest in Eastern Poland
And
Why didn't you buy gold at under 5k?

61999103
>>61999090
Is now a good time to buy? I was waiting for something like this, but I'm worried retards will short gold even more after I bought it.

Also Perth Mint seems to have closed gold buying. I can buy platinum while it's down for a low premium.

61999116
>>61999103
I was just being a retard on a dead board desu I'm short at 5k

61999136
>>61999116
I'm not sure what you mean by "short at 5k" because I don't do trading, I just periodically stack gold.

Are there any other investments you're in? America is now at $39 trillion in debt, so I expect a dollar crisis soon, so I would like to have as much gold as possible, but if there's a better investment I would be interest in that too temporarily.

61999149
>>61999136
It meand I sold (paper) gold I don't have for 5k an ounce with the plan of buying it back later at a cheaper price.

61999163
>>61999149
Ah, that's a good move. What price will you buy back in? I'm worried I just missed the bottom and my buying opportunity.

61999210
>>61998901
>Wtf is going on!?
LINK declared not a security.
We told you this was going to happen.

61999217
>>61999163
I have a trailing stop loss at this point so I'll buy when the selling momentum stops. I'm not a sophisticated investor.

61999225
>>61999217
Oh, that's sweet. Where do you plan on putting the money after?

I used most of my money to buy platinum at $1200, but I'm wondering if there's anything else to buy.

61999244
Gonna kms

61999259
>>61999244
Are you in Aus, NZ, or Singapore? You can give me your precious metals first, I would really appreciate it fren.

61999293
>>61999225
I'm scaling into a lot of short positions in shares. I feel like the price of volatility is too high rn for options. I like (or dislike rather) a lot of tech, such as rblx, zm, adbe.

61999302
>>61999293
Definitely short adobe, I'm a professional artist and that is the most dogshit company I've ever seen.

I've also been talking shit about Atlassian for 6 years. Glad to see it drop 80%.

Roblox and Zoom are also dogshit. I hope it goes well for you. I would also short them, but i don't really understand how that stuff works. I just like to buy and hold.

61999590
OMG! We are back to where we were Feb 6! Oh no!

62000062
>>61998888
>>61998901
And I hope it stays down a bit for my next DCA in April. My IAUI and BTCI will print money either way.

62000095
I dont think there are all but 3 stocks or commodities that have enjoyed the past 3 weeks tbqh

62000131
>>62000095
TLYS GEO SUN MUR MPC

62000238
>>62000095
Actually interesting post cause aside from the mentioned gas refiners (BP CVX) IT security (CRWD PANW NET) had a good month too. And COKE (distribution) for some reason even with rising fuel costs.

62000497
>>61998888
I just bought the dip. I'm gonna make it.

Also got some shiny silver. I wonder if It will rise enough just this year to cover the VAT.

62000674
>>61998901
Only short term holders are worried, stack sats and put it on babylon for yield while you wait for a new ATH. It's that simple

62000784
>>61998888
Nice quads. I used to be able to read PM's really well, it was honestly the only market I was really good at. But the last 6 months it has been behaving incredibly erratically and I'm sure I'm not the only one that find it very hard to predict where it is going.
